name: 13.22-create-22-reusable-call-workflow
description: Create the Workflow 22 template that calls the reusable title translator from another repository in the same organization.
model: GPT-5.5 (copilot)
---

# Create 22 Cross-Repository Caller Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/22-caller-workflow.yml` as an inspection and copy
template. Learners copy it to another repository in the same organization
before running it so the call crosses a real repository boundary.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a workflow named `22-reusable-call-workflow` that:

- Triggers only on manual dispatch so the inspection template does not run on
  source-repository pushes.
- Accepts `report_title`, `source_language`, and `target_language` inputs.
- Uses the Future is Faster Than You Think title as its default report title.
- Grants only read access to repository contents.
- Warns when source and target languages match.
- Calls `multi-layer-perceptron/ghcp-dotnet-calculator/.github/workflows/19-called-workflow.yml@main` at the job level.
- Passes all required inputs, including `${{ github.workflow }}` as `caller_workflow`.
- Displays the reusable workflow's `translated_title` output in a dependent job.
- States in comments that the committed file is a template that must run from a different repository in the same organization.

## Generation Requirements

1. Use exact basename `22-caller-workflow.yml`.
2. Keep the remote `owner/repository/path@ref` call syntax.
3. Do not add secrets or write permissions.
4. Pass input-derived values to shell commands through environment variables.
5. Keep validation in Workflow 19 rather than duplicating it in the caller.
6. Explain that `@main` is mutable and should be replaced with a release tag or full commit SHA for durable use.
7. Do not claim the call is cross-repository when the template runs from this repository.

## Validation

Run `actionlint` against Workflows 19 and 22 when available. Verify the caller
repository and called repository Actions access settings before a live test.
